mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of a method in Java?

options.text: To declare variables
To define behaviors that objects can perform
To create objects
To assign object reference variables

mcqs.explanation: Methods define behaviors that objects can perform, allowing objects to take actions and interact with other objects.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between pass-by-value and pass-by-reference in Java?

options.text: Pass-by-value creates a copy of the original variable, while pass-by-reference passes the original variable
Pass-by-value passes the original variable, while pass-by-reference creates a copy of the original variable
Pass-by-value is used for primitives, while pass-by-reference is used for objects
Pass-by-value is used for objects, while pass-by-reference is used for primitives

mcqs.explanation: In Java, pass-by-value creates a copy of the original variable. For objects, a copy of the reference value is passed.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'static' keyword in Java?

options.text: To declare instance variables
To declare static variables
To declare methods that belong to the class
To declare methods that belong to an object

mcqs.explanation: The 'static' keyword is used to declare methods that belong to the class, rather than an instance of the class.

mcqs.question: Can a static method access instance variables in Java?

options.text: Yes
No
Only if the instance variable is static
Only if the instance variable is final

mcqs.explanation: A static method cannot access instance variables because it does not have an instance of the class to access.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'void' keyword in Java?

options.text: To declare methods that return a value
To declare methods that do not return a value
To declare variables that are not initialized
To declare variables that are initialized

mcqs.explanation: The 'void' keyword is used to declare methods that do not return a value.

mcqs.question: What is the method signature in Java?

options.text: The method name and return type
The method name and parameter types
The method name, return type, and parameter types
The method name, parameter types, and access modifier

mcqs.explanation: The method signature in Java consists of the method name and parameter types.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between an instance method and a static method in Java?

options.text: An instance method belongs to the class, while a static method belongs to an object
An instance method belongs to an object, while a static method belongs to the class
An instance method can access static variables, while a static method cannot
A static method can access instance variables, while an instance method cannot

mcqs.explanation: An instance method belongs to an object, while a static method belongs to the class.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'this' keyword in Java?

options.text: To refer to the current object
To refer to the current class
To declare instance variables
To declare static variables

mcqs.explanation: The 'this' keyword is used to refer to the current object.
